tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function with possible metabolic acidosis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones) indicate renal involvement, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal and metabolic status.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
